official_sources.title: Submit metrics — Datadog API Reference
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/v2/series contract: DD-API-KEY auth, Content-Type application/json, optional Content-Encoding, series schema (metric, integer type enum 0=unspecified/1=count/2=rate/3=gauge, points as {timestamp,value} objects, resources {name,type}, tags, unit, interval for rate/count), point freshness (not >10min future, not >1h past), payload limits (512000 bytes; compressed payloads must decompress to <5242880 bytes), 202 {"errors":[]} success and 400/403/408/413/429 error envelope

official_sources.title: Getting Started with Datadog Sites
official_sources.used_for: site parameter values (datadoghq.com, us3.datadoghq.com, us5.datadoghq.com, datadoghq.eu, ap1.datadoghq.com, ap2.datadoghq.com, ddog-gov.com) and that each independent site is reached at https://api.<site>

official_sources.title: Rate Limits — Datadog API Reference
official_sources.used_for: X-RateLimit-Limit/Period/Remaining/Reset/Name header family, 429 semantics and retry guidance; note that metric point submission itself is not rate limited

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-python MetricContentEncoding model (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: the exact allowed Content-Encoding values for v2 metric submission: deflate, zstd1, gzip

research.language_rationale: The metrics pipeline this submitter replaces is Python. Datadog's official datadog-api-client-python is a generated wrapper over this same v2 REST endpoint, and the verify environment is stdlib-only, so urllib.request against the documented POST /api/v2/series contract is the credible dependency-free integration surface; the REST reference is Datadog's primary documented interface.

verified_facts: Metric submission is POST /api/v2/series authenticated by the DD-API-KEY header alone; an application key is not part of this operation.

The v2 series schema uses an integer metric type enum (0 unspecified, 1 count, 2 rate, 3 gauge), points as {timestamp, value} objects, and resources as {name, type} objects; interval is documented for rate and count metrics.
Point timestamps must be at most 10 minutes in the future and at most 1 hour in the past.
The maximum payload size is 500 kilobytes (512000 bytes); a compressed payload must have a decompressed size below 5 megabytes (5242880 bytes).
Content-Encoding for compressed submission may be deflate, gzip, or zstd1 per the official client's MetricContentEncoding model.
Success is 202 with an {"errors": []} body; failures reuse the same errors-array envelope with statuses including 400, 403, 408, 413, and 429.
Datadog rate limiting is surfaced through 429 responses plus the X-RateLimit-Limit/Period/Remaining/Reset/Name headers; a Retry-After style wait then retry is the documented recovery.
Datadog sites are independent (datadoghq.com, us3, us5, datadoghq.eu, ap1, ap2, ddog-gov.com) and the API host for a site is https://api.<site>.
